terms.definition: High-level plotly.express (px) API that creates interactive figures from DataFrame column mappings—scatter, line, histogram, bar, imshow, and 3D variants with one function call.
Low-level plotly.graph_objects (go) module where traces like go.Scatter and go.Bar are added explicitly to a go.Figure for full layout and trace control.
A single data series in a Plotly figure—one Scatter, Bar, or Heatmap object—toggleable in the legend and styled independently.
String template controlling tooltip content using placeholders such as %{x}, %{y}, and %{customdata[i]} for per-point metadata in ML plots.
plotly.subplots function that builds multi-panel figures with row/column placement and optional shared axes for aligned metric views.
Figure method that serializes an interactive plot to a standalone HTML file viewable in any browser without a Jupyter kernel.
